title: The Legend of Zelda: Majora's Mask
review: Whenever there is a meeting, a parting shall follow. But that parting needs not last forever. Whether a parting be forever or merely for a short while... that is up to you. The Legend of Zelda: Majora’s Mask taught little Daniel a lot of lessons about friendship, grief, and time. What seems like a children’s game on the outside, tells a lot of heavy stories we can all reflect upon. What would I do when the world is about to end? Who would I spend my last hours with? It’s scary to think about but MM told me that we shouldn’t be scared but be happy that it happened. Everyone in Clocktown feels so real due to the inventive day and night cycle Nintendo has implemented in the game. We see their daily lives and how they react to incoming death. As the player, we get to rewind time and see all the lives of Clocktown and I feel like that’s the true beauty of this game. The melancholy music, the beautiful and at times eerie atmosphere, and the stories of the characters are the reasons why Majora’s Mask is a cult classic. Without a doubt in my mind, Majora’s Mask is a BYTE!

title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
review: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is the most memorable game I have played in the last few years. Created by indie dev studio Sandfall Interactive, this game had me from the moment I booted up the main menu. I have always been a fan of classical and operatic music and Lorien Testard gave his life's work on this game. The soundtrack alone is worth game of the year. Once I started my playthrough, the cutscenes and visuals grabbed me immediately. The tutorial section of the game alone drew a tear out of my eyes and left me hungry for more. What kept me engaged beyond the presentation was the combat. I have never seen turn-based combat this interactive. It keeps you in the fight every single second and never once felt unfair. With the beautiful cutscenes and graphics, gorgeous soundtrack, and unique gameplay I have never seen before, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 is undoubtedly a BYTE.

title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
review: The LoZ franchise hit a brick wall with their formula feeling too repetitive. That all changed when Nintendo took a 180 degree turn and completely changed their iconic formula into something grander than ever. The tutorial section alone of BotW is the single best tutorial ever made for a video game. The Great Plateau is able to teach players critical mechanics of the game while making sure the player does not feel overwhelmed. Once you get paragliding down from the Great Plateau you realize how massive this game truly is. What’s amazing is how everything in the game is optional. You are able to defeat the final boss without completing any other story content. While you play, you come to realize that the game is YOUR story. You are the one in control the whole time, writing your own start and conclusion to your epic. The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild is a BIG BYTE!

title: Resident Evil 2 (2019)
review: Resident Evil 2 Remake is a remake done right. When remakes like Final Fantasy 7 like to prioritize stretching out the game into three parts and completely changing the battle mechanic to target a bigger audience, RE2 tries to stay faithful as much as possible. Remaking a game is difficult as it's not just trying to preserve the game one-to-one but rather trying to show a reimagination where it also doesn't strike too far from the original. RE2 strikes this balance well by keeping the general story beats the same while upgrading mechanics, graphics, and shifting locations of items so it still feels fresh to veterans. RE2 doesn't change the combat and perspective mechanics too much where it strays from the original, but it changes it from old tank controls to a 3rd person perspective. It essentially reintroduces their other game's mechanics into RE2 which makes it feel like it's how the game was envisioned from the start of the original production. The game is quite short and I feel like more could've been done with the second playthrough of the game. I beat it in one night and although I really enjoyed it I was left feeling like there could've been more inclusions to the game while also staying faithful to the source material. I give Resident Evil 2 Remake 7bits out of Byte.

title: Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six Siege
review: Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six Siege is where I was introduced to e-sports. It fueled my competitive drive and made me become the person I am today. I played this game for hours on end and even when I quit, I took the same mentality for other competitive FPS games and reached the highest rank in whatever I played. If it weren't for R6, I wouldn't have competed in tournaments for other games and gotten the experience of professional esports. The gameplay is uniquely designed unlike any other FPS I've played, but recent updates have made the game a lot less fun. Overall, I give it a 6bits out of Byte.

title: Dark Souls III
review: Dark Souls III is definitely my favorite Soulslike game of all time. Out of all FromSoftware games I think that DS3 has perfectly balanced the difficulty in a way where it’s earned from the enemy’s standpoint. Every death gives a learning experience where the answer isn’t just BS. That does not make it any easier than other Soulslike games as it’s still one of the most challenging. The music and the bosses are the best out of any Soulslike game I’ve ever played. The ruined-world atmosphere is absolutely beautiful and sometimes terrifying. Undead Settlement gave me the most quiet but terrifying feeling throughout my adventure. Once I got to the Twin Princes, I knew this game was the best out of the whole series. Overall I give this game a BYTE!

title: Resident Evil 7: Biohazard
review: When the survival horror genre was dying out, Resident Evil 7 marked the great revival for the genre. Capcom overhauled their formula and created a new unique style of resident evil we hadn't seen before. We were introduced to a new character, Ethan Winters, we are playing on the new REEngine that drastically improved visuals, and the first-person perspective. Everything that people didn’t like about Resident Evil 6 was removed and Capcom returned to the classic Resident Evil formula. The dark, moldy, and broken home aesthetic is well done and definitely kept me on my toes my whole playthrough. The only downside of RE7 is the lack of monsters we face during our playthrough. The mold monsters are scary at first but then every single other monster we face are molded variants which we eventually get numb to. Although the lack of diverse monsters is disappointing, the gameplay, scare factor, and the impact it has made on the survival horror genre is massive. I give it a 7bits out of Byte!

title: Valorant
review: I have a deep love and hate relationship with Valorant. I hit the highest rank and competed in small tournaments for it, but I find myself extremely frustrated all the time from playing this game. The game excels at rewarding skilled play and feels like the most fair out of all competitive esport titles I have played. What lacks in the game is how sluggish the movement feels even after 2000 hours of playing and the direction of recent updates. It seems like Riot doesn't have a clue on how to expand the game more as the new maps and characters introduced are just copies of the past. Even though I have played this game the most out of any titles, I can only safely say its 5bits out of Byte.

title: Resident Evil Village
review: After the release of Resident Evil 7: Biohazard, people have been expecting another slow-burning scary horror game. What we got instead is Resident Evil Village, and I am NOT complaining. After the classic Texas Chainsaw Massacre setting RE7 implemented, we moved to a Transylvania setting. Compared to the trapped home feeling RE7 gives you, RE8 feels a lot more expansive as we get to roam a whole (I know) village. This works as an advantage for RE8 as a major complaint for RE7 was how stale the game gets visually towards the end. In RE8 we get to explore a Vampire's castle, rotting fish village, creepy doll home, and factory. The game masters the pacing of the player as the first few levels of the game are slow and terrifying, but as the game progresses the game turns more into a badass shooting range. I feel like what RE8 tried to achieve here was not another RE7, but a game that we will remember as RE8. Capcom is not trying to create another clone of their games and are trying to reinvent their formulas by getting their old formula and adding twists. Overall, Resident Evil Village is 6bits out of Byte.

title: Bioshock Infinite
review: Bioshock Infinite takes the game's deep ocean setting into the sky. The beautiful graphics not only hold up but are still impressive today in 2026. I still vividly remember the first time I was sent into the sky and saw Columbia with the beautiful music playing in the background. The early 20th century Americana setting is well done and I was fully immersed in the gorgeous fairs and barbershop quartet music in the background. But like all Bioshock games, we start to see the holes in these societies. The game’s story is ambitious but it feels like it falls flat compared to previous entries. A lot of plot points feel hollow, scrapped, or rushed. This was probably due to the nightmarish development cycle the game went through. Even still, the game turned out great and I still come back to the beautiful world of Columbia from time to time. 7bits out of Byte.

title: Super Mario Galaxy 2
review: Super Mario Galaxy 2 is the first game I’ve ever played. I was only 6 years old when the game came out and I still remember vividly exploring the different galaxies in the game. The gravity mechanic in the game is amazing and gives the game a refreshing version of Mario we hadn’t seen before. The graphics of the game still hold up after 16 years and only required an upscale for future remastered releases. The amount of attention and care that was put into Super Mario Galaxy 2 gives it a deserving BYTE!

title: Final Fantasy VII Remake
review: To an OG Final Fantasy fan, FF7 Remake is an insult. Although the graphics are beautiful and the story is expanded upon, the gameplay is a huge downgrade from the original game. It would’ve genuinely been better if the game was just a cutscene montage, but the bloated minigames and mediocre gameplay is just not my style. At many times it feels like the game is padding up unnecessary sections of the game just to justify the three part launch of the game. 4 bits out of Byte.

title: Detroit: Become Human
review: Quantic Dream perfected their formula with Detroit: Become Human. A beautiful story game where every decision you make can impact the outcome of the story. A lot of games love to claim that every action in their game impacts the result of the story, but none of them come to the level of Detroit: Become Human. The narrative for every character you play as and endings you get are intertwined seamlessly. I was shocked how complicated the narrative system was compared to what I expected from a story game. The visual tree system they implemented in this game is stunning and makes the player want to go deeper into the game and try to get all the endings. The system is great, but if the story sucks no one wants to play it. Thankfully Detroit has a thrilling and emotional story that kept me attached to the characters the whole way through. The one drawback of this game though is that sometimes the interactive buttons feel pointless and feels like it could’ve been expanded upon. In conclusion, Detroit: Become Human is a STRONG 7bits out of Byte!

title: Hades
review: What seems like out of nowhere, Hades came and crushed the expectations people had for it. The combat, OMG the COMBAT is SO ADDICTING. Hades is a roguelike game where you need to escape Hell in order to be with the Gods above. At first the game is very difficult and you most likely won’t be able to succeed in your escape. You are able to upgrade your stats and weapons based on the currency you get from your attempts to escape hell. With those attributes each escape not only becomes more fresh and fun, but it also becomes a lot manageable. Hades was great at keeping me engaged every run without making it feel repetitive. For that, I give Hades a BYTE!

title: Undertale
review: Undertale holds a special place in my heart. It is the first game I've ever gotten a 100% completion on. Toby Fox is a genius who is able to create fantastic music that is still used in many forms of content today, create a story where you fall in love with all the characters, and create a battle system we have never seen before. Undertale has told a young me that killing is not the best thing to do in every situation (obviously). Even when someone is out to get us, we should try to find a middle ground or fight with love. Undertale is a strong BYTE for me.

title: Payday 3
review: Absolute Downgrade from Payday 2. Terrible, -10bits out of Byte.
